market_session: BEA release 2026-06-25 08:30 ET; market context through 2026-06-26 US session
text: The BEA reported that the May 2026 PCE price index rose 0.4% month over month and 4.1% from a year earlier, while core PCE rose 0.3% month over month and 3.4% year over year. Reuters and CNBC described the core reading as the highest since October 2023 and the headline annual reading as the highest since April 2023.

market_session: BEA release 2026-06-25 08:30 ET; market context through 2026-06-26 US session
text: The May income and spending release showed nominal demand still firm: BEA reported personal income up 0.7%, disposable personal income up 0.7%, current-dollar PCE up 0.7%, and real PCE up 0.3%, while the saving rate was 3.0% and personal saving was $704.2 billion.

Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari said he changed his outlook from one rate cut by year-end to one rate hike by year-end, citing inflation pressure from Middle East supply risk, tariffs, fertilizer disruption, energy and oil prices, and massive data-center investment. CNBC also noted inflation remained above the Fed's 2% goal.

market_session: Friday 2026-06-26 US rates session, CNBC table and Reuters global-markets wrap
text: Treasury yields eased in the front end and belly despite hot inflation data: CNBC's Friday table showed the 10-year yield at 4.374% (-0.018), the 2-year at 4.088% (-0.033), and the 30-year at 4.866% (+0.007). Reuters separately reported the benchmark 10-year yield down 1.75 basis points to 4.375%.

Reuters reported Brent crude futures down 4.24% to $72.07 per barrel as Hormuz supply concerns eased, the dollar index down 0.3% to 101.20 but headed for a second weekly gain, USD/JPY near 161.62 and close to 40-year yen lows, spot gold up 1.5% to $4,086.29, and the German 10-year Bund yield down 0.76 basis point to 2.852%.

The University of Michigan final June survey showed sentiment rising to 49.5 from 44.8 in May, still 18.5% below June 2025. Year-ahead inflation expectations slipped to 4.6% from 4.8%, while long-run expectations fell to 3.3% from 3.9%, leaving inflation expectations elevated even as gasoline-price relief helped sentiment.

Global risk tone was tech-led and defensive: Reuters reported MSCI's world-stock gauge down 0.34% and set for a 2% weekly loss, European shares down nearly 0.9% with technology down 1.54%, MSCI Asia ex-Japan down nearly 3%, and chip stocks down 4.5% on the day and set to lose 7% for the week.
